name: Ermita de la Antigua and Río Iregua Loop
name_original: Ermita de la Antigua desde Alberite — circular por el río Iregua
description: Enjoy an easy 4.1-mile circular hike from Alberite along the Río Iregua, featuring the historic Hermitage of Our Lady of the Antigua.
region_intro: Hiking around Alberite, a municipality in La Rioja, Spain, offers diverse landscapes for outdoor exploration. The region features vineyard-covered hills, river valleys, and mountainous terrain, providing varied elevations and natural beauty. Hikers can experience the blend of agricultural scenery with more rugged paths, often encountering historical elements and natural water features. The area is characterized by its distinct topography, ranging from gentle riverside paths to more challenging ascents. Best hiking trails around Alberite The most popular hiking route is Peña Clavijo, Church of Our Lady of the Assumption, Ruins of the Monastery of San Prudencio – circular route, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 2 minutes to complete. This route offers views of Peña Clavijo and historical ruins. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ermita de la Antigua and Río Iregua Loop, an easy 4.1 miles (6.6 km) path. This trail follows the Iregua River, providing riverside scenery and access to a local hermitage. Local hikers also love the Murillo de Río Leza, a 3.2 miles (5.2 km) trail leading through river landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 27 minutes. Hiking around Alberite is defined by vineyard landscapes, river valleys, and hills, with options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails. The network offers options for different ability levels. The routes in Alberite are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2,300 hikers have used komoot to explore Alberite's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Alberite?
A: Alberite offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with nearly 180 routes recorded on komoot. These trails cater to different fitness levels, including 60 easy, 110 moderate, and 9 difficult options.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking near Alberite?
A: Hiking around Alberite reveals diverse landscapes, from extensive vineyard-covered hills, particularly in La Rioja Alta, to rugged mountainous terrain in areas like the Sierra de Cameros. You'll also find river valleys, unique reddish rock formations in La Rioja Baja, and tranquil water bodies such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/682737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Grajera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails in Alberite?
A: Yes, Alberite has several easy trails suitable for families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1327649206/ermita-de-la-antigua-and-rio-iregua-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de la Antigua and Río Iregua Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6.6 km path that follows the Iregua River, offering pleasant riverside scenery. The Urbión Lagoons, mentioned in the broader La Rioja region, also provide family-friendly routes through a glacial landscape.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Alberite?
A: Yes, many routes around Alberite are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989672570/pena-clavijo-church-of-our-lady-of-the-assumption-ruins-of-the-monastery-of-san-prudencio-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Clavijo, Church of Our Lady of the Assumption, Ruins of the Monastery of San Prudencio – circular route&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.3 km loop offering historical views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38258647" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Navarrete – La Grajera Reservoir loop from Estadio Municipal Las Gaunas&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 17.5 km trail that circles the reservoir.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or significant water features to see on hikes near Alberite?
A: While Alberite itself is more known for vineyards and river valleys, the broader La Rioja region features impressive natural water sites. The Natural Park of the Sierra de Cebollera, for instance, is home to the picturesque waterfall of Puente Ra and the canyon of the Leza River, offering beautiful forest and water-themed trails.

Q: What historical landmarks or viewpoints can I discover on trails around Alberite?
A: Hiking trails around Alberite often lead to historical sites and offer scenic viewpoints. You can explore the ruins of the Monastery of San Prudencio on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989672570/pena-clavijo-church-of-our-lady-of-the-assumption-ruins-of-the-monastery-of-san-prudencio-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Clavijo circular route&lt;/a&gt;. The famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4020897" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Logroño Stone Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, part of the Camino Francés, is also accessible via routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6953595" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Logroño Stone Bridge – Iregua Riverside Park loop&lt;/a&gt;. Villages like Cellorigo offer breathtaking panoramic views of La Rioja Alta.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Alberite?
A: The trails in Alberite are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2,300 hikers have explored the area, often praising the varied terrain, from gentle riverside paths to more challenging ascents, and the blend of agricultural scenery with natural beauty.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alberite?
A: La Rioja, where Alberite is located, offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring, after the mountain thaw, is ideal for seeing crystalline waters in areas like the Urbión Lagoons. The vineyard landscapes are particularly colorful in autumn. Summers can be warm, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable, while winters offer a different, often quieter, experience.

Q: Are there any trails that follow rivers or offer riverside scenery?
A: Yes, several trails in the area follow rivers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1327649206/ermita-de-la-antigua-and-rio-iregua-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de la Antigua and Río Iregua Loop&lt;/a&gt; follows the Iregua River, providing scenic riverside views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1327377230/murillo-de-rio-leza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Murillo de Río Leza&lt;/a&gt; trail, which leads through river landscapes along the Leza River.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by vineyards?
A: Absolutely. The region of La Rioja is famous for its vineyards, and many trails around Alberite traverse these landscapes. La Rioja Alta, in particular, is dominated by vineyards, offering unique and colorful scenery, especially around areas like San Vicente de la Sonsierra, which provides views of the Rioja vineyards and the Ebro River.

Q: What is the difficulty level of hiking trails around Alberite?
A: Alberite offers a range of difficulty levels to suit various hikers. You can find 60 easy trails, 110 moderate routes, and 9 more challenging options. This variety ensures that both casual walkers and experienced hikers can find suitable paths, from gentle riverside strolls to more demanding ascents in the hills.
tips: Peaceful and quiet outdoor park on the banks of the Iregua River, on the outskirts of Alberite. Enjoy a walk in this beautiful landscape as the seasons change.

The Hermitage of contemporary architecture, made of concrete, can be seen on the outskirts of the town of Alberite in La Rioja. The architectural style hoped to establish a connection between the structure, the personal scale and the surrounding landscape. You can be the judge of that.
A small town on the banks of the Iregua River, south of Logroño. From afar you can see the bell tower of the town church.

faqs.question: How long does the circular hike typically take?
faqs.answer: This easy circular hike, covering approximately 6.56 kilometers, typically takes about 1 hour and 49 minutes to complete. This duration allows for a relaxed pace to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is classified as easy and is suitable for all fitness levels, including beginners and families. It features easily accessible paths and a relatively low elevation gain, making it a pleasant outdoor experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, offering a mix of surfaces. You'll encounter sections of gravel (2.10 km), asphalt (1.47 km), and paved sections (1.23 km), along with unpaved paths (280 m). The route primarily follows designated hiking paths (2.10 km) and general paths (2.84 km), with some portions on streets and a small mountain hiking path.

faqs.question: What notable sights will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several points of interest. You'll hike along the tranquil banks of the Río Iregua, enjoying the rural charm of its valley. A significant stop is the Hermitage of Our Lady of the Antigua of Alberite, a 15th-century hermitage with a unique modern restoration. The town of Alberite also features the historical Church of San Martín.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Alberite. While specific parking details aren't provided, Alberite is the main access point, and parking can typically be found within the town or near the starting point of the circular route.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas or near wildlife, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is particularly vibrant in the spring when the fruit trees in the Iregua river valley are in full bloom. However, its easy nature and varied terrain make it enjoyable throughout most of the year, weather permitting. The peaceful outdoor environment along the river is pleasant in many seasons.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to hike this circular route to the Ermita de la Antigua from Alberite along the Río Iregua.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that are part of the Vía Romana del Iregua, Camino de los Vinateros, and Cuesta de las Piscinas, among others. This offers a glimpse into the broader network of trails in the region.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink near the trail?
faqs.answer: Alberite, the starting and ending point of the hike, is a town nestled in the heart of the Iregua valley. You can expect to find local cafes and restaurants in Alberite where you can refresh yourself before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What makes the Ermita de la Antigua a significant stop on this hike?
faqs.answer: The Hermitage of Our Lady of the Antigua of Alberite is a 15th-century structure that underwent a notable restoration in 2009. The architect skillfully integrated its original elements with a modern concrete design, creating a unique blend of old and new. Inside, a transferred altarpiece and a medieval arch add to its historical and architectural appeal, while its design thoughtfully frames the surrounding landscape.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Our Lady of the Antigua of Alberite
